title: Seven years after arson attack on Kyoto Animation, floral tribute held at studio
summary: Relatives, employees, and fans pay homage to victims of arson that left 36 dead.

title: Suspect arrested for abandoning bodies of girlfriend and daughter in Nara mountains
summary: The suspect confessed to transporting the corpses in his own vehicle and abandoning them in a remote area.

title: Roki Sasaki impresses with 164 km/h fastball and holds Yankees to one hit
summary: The young Japanese pitcher allowed one run in six innings and showed a powerful pitching repertoire.

title: World Cup final set between Argentina and Spain, third place match between France and England
summary: The tournament's final matches are held amid debate over excessive commercialisation and political interference.

title: Torrential rains in Tochigi and Gunma cause landslides and collapsed house, two missing
summary: An elderly couple is trapped under debris after mudslide caused by heavy rainfall.

title: Apple raises iPhone price in Japan by 10% due to weak yen and rising chip costs
summary: The US company applies the increase to its latest models, citing economic and cost factors.

title: Hunter survives brown bear attack in Hokkaido after injuring it in the eyes
summary: The 40-year-old man managed to defend himself from the animal, and the bear was later shot by local authorities.

title: Japanese and US foreign ministers attend ASEAN meetings to curb China's influence
summary: The diplomatic meeting seeks to strengthen regional cooperation and counter Beijing's growing aspirations in Southeast Asia.

title: Wildfires in Canada cause record pollution and threaten World Cup final
summary: Smoke reaches areas of the United States and President Trump proposes imposing tariffs as retaliation for the disaster.

title: US strikes Iran for seventh consecutive day and oil surpasses $80
summary: The military escalation increases tension in the Middle East and puts global crude supply at risk.

title: Japan enacts law criminalizing desecration of national flag amid criticism
summary: The opposition and civil rights experts consider the rule to infringe on freedom of expression.

title: China strengthens influence with SLBM missile launch, summit with North Korea, and new AI initiative
summary: Beijing deploys a multidimensional strategy that includes military, diplomatic, and technological advances globally.

title: Debate on imperial succession gains momentum with proposals for adoption and female succession
summary: The discussions aim to ensure stability of the succession line in the face of a shortage of male heirs.

title: Hideki Matsuyama and Naoyuki Kataoka advance at the British Open golf tournament
summary: Both Japanese golfers make the cut at the major, with Matsuyama climbing to 38th place.

title: Continued delays of maglev train in Japan criticized after a decade of construction
summary: The project, which includes the country's deepest tunnel, lacks a definitive completion schedule.

title: JR West seeks to bring forward fare hike while government pushes Western Kyushu Shinkansen extension
summary: The railway operator requests an early increase due to financial difficulties, while authorities seek to unblock the project.

title: Wall Street closes lower on tech sell-offs, but Nikkei rises in futures
summary: The Dow Jones loses 406 points over fears of a correction in the AI sector, while the Japanese market shows signs of recovery.

title: US considers creating independent body to oversee artificial intelligence
summary: In parallel, Meta negotiates a massive data center agreement with Anthropic and China presents a competitive AI model.

title: Hiroshima Carp outfielder Yano implicated in scandal and removed from roster
summary: The club decides to temporarily separate him from the team after his presence was revealed at a meeting with an illegal tobacco user.

title: Japan considers new nuclear plants and public financing for atomic energy sector
summary: Faced with increasing electricity demand, the government promotes reactor construction and amends the electricity law.
